Atlanta Gas Price Forecast Report

Generated: 2026-03-01 12:53:40

1. Executive Summary

Atlanta_Forecast = Kalshi_National_Forward + Predicted_Spread

This model forecasts Atlanta-area retail gasoline prices by combining Kalshi prediction market implied forward prices for the national average with a machine-learning-predicted Atlanta-National spread (PADD 1C Lower Atlantic minus U.S. average).

RandomForest
Best Model
$0.0108
Test RMSE ($/gal)
$0.0080
Test MAE ($/gal)
0.877
Test R²
The best model (RandomForest) was selected on the validation set and then evaluated on a held-out test set of 52 weeks that were never seen during training or model selection.

2. Best Model: How It Works

Random Forest is an ensemble of decision trees, each trained on a random bootstrap sample of the data using a random subset of features. The final prediction is the average of all trees.

This "wisdom of crowds" approach reduces overfitting compared to a single tree while maintaining interpretability through feature importance scores.

Key hyperparameters: n_estimators=300, max_depth=8, min_samples_leaf=5

3. Variables & Data Sources

The model uses 35 features derived from EIA petroleum data, computed time-series features, and Kalshi market data.

4. Kalshi Integration

Kalshi operates binary prediction markets on U.S. gasoline prices. Each contract asks: "Will the national average gas price be above $X.XX?" and trades at a price between 1¢ and 99¢ reflecting the market-implied probability.

How Binary Contracts Become Forward Prices

Given a set of contracts at different strikes for the same expiration:

  1. Extract probabilities: Each contract's yes_price / 100 gives P(price > strike).
  2. Bucket probabilities: P(price in [Si, Si+1)) = P(above Si) - P(above Si+1).
  3. Expected value: E[Price] = Σ midpointi × Pi.

Worked Example

Suppose contracts for next month:

StrikeYes PriceP(above)
$3.0085¢0.85
$3.2555¢0.55
$3.5020¢0.20

Bucket [$3.00, $3.25): P = 0.85 - 0.55 = 0.30, mid = $3.125
Bucket [$3.25, $3.50): P = 0.55 - 0.20 = 0.35, mid = $3.375
Below $3.00: P = 0.15, mid ~ $2.95
Above $3.50: P = 0.20, mid ~ $3.55

E[Price] = 0.15×2.95 + 0.30×3.125 + 0.35×3.375 + 0.20×3.55 = $3.23

Monthly vs Weekly

Monthly contracts (KXAAAGASM) settle on the EIA monthly average and provide a smoother term structure. Weekly contracts (KXAAAGASW) settle on each weekly EIA release and capture near-term price dynamics with higher time resolution but potentially thinner liquidity.

Monthly forwards: 23 expiration(s), price range $2.844 - $3.570

Weekly forwards: 11 expiration(s), price range $2.815 - $3.730

5. Data Split & Evaluation

SplitRowsDate RangePurpose
Train310 2018-03-26 to 2024-02-26 Fit model parameters
Validation52 2024-03-04 to 2025-02-24 Select best model, tune ensemble weights
Test52 2025-03-03 to 2026-02-23 Final holdout — true out-of-sample metrics
Key principle: The test set was never used for any model selection or hyperparameter tuning. Metrics on the test set reflect genuine out-of-sample performance.

Test Set Summary (RandomForest)

Detailed holdout analysis on 52 test weeks (2025-03-03 to 2026-02-23). These weeks were never seen during training or model selection.

When were these predictions made? Each weekly spread prediction uses features from the prior week (e.g., spread_lag1, last week’s refinery utilization, last week’s WTI price change). So each row is effectively a ~7-day-ahead forecast: given data through last Monday, predict this Monday’s Atlanta–National spread. The “Predicted $/gal” column uses the actual national retail price (not a Kalshi forward) plus the predicted spread — see the Monthly Analysis tab for full end-to-end forecasts using Kalshi forwards.
$0.0108
RMSE
Average error magnitude in $/gal. Lower is better.
$0.0080
MAE
Typical miss size. The model is off by about 0.8 cents on average.
0.877
R²
How much variance the model explains. 1.0 = perfect, 0 = no better than guessing the mean.
86.3%
Direction Accuracy
How often the model correctly predicts whether the spread goes up or down week-to-week.
$0.0344
Max Error
Largest single-week miss in the entire test set.
-0.0012
Mean Bias
The model systematically under-predicts by ~0.1 cents/gal.

Kalshi Brier Score

The Brier score measures how well Kalshi market prices predicted actual outcomes on settled contracts. A score of 0.0 means perfect calibration; 0.25 is what you’d get from always guessing 50/50; 1.0 is the worst possible.

0.0138
Brier Score (Monthly)
196 settled contracts. 0 = perfect, 0.25 = uninformed baseline.
0.0228
Brier Score (Weekly)
138 settled contracts. 0 = perfect, 0.25 = uninformed baseline.

How This Tab Works

This is a true ~30-day-ahead forecast. A separate XGBoost model was trained specifically to predict the monthly average Atlanta–National spread using only data available at the end of the prior month. This means: on the last day of Month N, the model predicts what the average spread will be during Month N+1. The features include the last known spread, rolling averages, prior-month exogenous data (WTI, refinery utilization, stocks), and seasonal indicators for the target month.

Each row is one calendar month from the model’s test period:

  • Prediction made — End of the prior month (using only data available at that point).
  • Kalshi Forward — The market-implied national avg gas price for that month (from Kalshi binary contracts).
  • Predicted Spread — The monthly model’s ~30-day-ahead spread prediction (Atlanta minus National).
  • Pred Atlanta (E2E) — Full end-to-end: Kalshi Forward + Monthly Predicted Spread.
  • Actual Atlanta — Real monthly avg PADD 1C retail price.
  • E2E Error — Predicted minus Actual. Positive = over-predicted.

How well did Kalshi’s implied forward prices predict the actual national average?

Kalshi MAE

Kalshi Bias

Note: Kalshi forward prices shown here are derived from the last available traded prices on settled contracts. Early in a contract’s life, the market may have priced differently. This comparison shows how close the final market consensus was to the actual outcome.
